rcomplex 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Comparative co-expression analysis with rcomplex

Classify modules by conservation status

Source: R/modules.R
classify_modules.Rd

Classifies each module as conserved, partially conserved, or species-specific based on cross-species module comparison results.

classify_modules(module_comparison, alpha = 0.05, jaccard_threshold = 0.1)

Arguments

module_comparison

Output of compare_modules().

alpha

Significance threshold for q-values (default 0.05).

jaccard_threshold

Minimum Jaccard index to call a module conserved (default 0.1).

Value

A data frame with one row per module (from both species), including:

module

Module identifier

species

Which species this module belongs to ("sp1" or "sp2")

classification

One of "conserved", "partially_conserved", or "species_specific"

best_match

Best matching module in the other species

best_jaccard

Jaccard index with best match

best_q

Q-value for best match

n_significant

Number of significant matches in the other species

Classification criteria

Conserved

Best-match q-value < alpha AND Jaccard >= jaccard_threshold

Partially conserved

Best-match q-value < alpha AND Jaccard < jaccard_threshold (module split or merged across species)

Species-specific

No significant match (best-match q-value >= alpha)

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
classes <- classify_modules(mod_comp)
table(classes$classification)  # conserved / partially / species_specific
} # }
